records.ety: [L. obligatus, p. p. of obligare. See Oblige.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Obligated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Obligating.]

defs: To bring or place under obligation, moral or legal; to hold by a constraining motive.
quotes: That's your true plan -- to obligate The present ministers of state.

defs: To bind or firmly hold to an act; to compel; to constrain; to bind to any act of duty or courtesy by a formal pledge.
quotes: That they may not incline or be obligated to any vile or lowly occupations.
